expectations.text: Flags service.ts direct container.db query instead of the repository
expectations.evidence: Finding 3: 'service queries the DB directly, bypassing its repository ... issuing a raw Drizzle query and importing the DB schema.'

expectations.text: Flags adapter instantiated in service (new StripeClient)
expectations.evidence: Finding 1: 'the Application layer should depend on an injected adapter obtained from the container, not construct a concrete Infrastructure class.'
Finding 1: 'LocalSecretsProvider is the only place allowed to touch process.env.'

expectations.text: Flags raw Drizzle row ($inferSelect) leaking out, missing toDomain
expectations.evidence: No mention of InvoiceRow/$inferSelect leaking or a missing toDomain mapper. Finding 6 only discusses Zod/TS type drift and shared-package placement, not the row-type leak.

expectations.text: Flags route bypassing service via new BillingRepository
expectations.evidence: Finding 2: 'Instantiating a repository (Infrastructure) directly ... Presentation should never skip the Application layer.'

expectations.text: Flags business logic (dunning tier loop/if-else) in the route
expectations.evidence: Finding 2: 'Computing the amount-due total and the dunning-tier thresholds — that's domain/application logic living in a route handler.'

expectations.text: Flags manual workspaceId from headers instead of getContext
expectations.evidence: Demoted to a Minor/nit: 'x-workspace-id is read and cast as string ... Consider a shared header schema / preHandler.' Never mentions getContext; not treated as an architecture violation.

expectations.text: Flags cross-module import of AgentsRepository in the repository
expectations.evidence: Finding 4: 'billing repo instantiates another module's repository (cross-module coupling at the wrong layer).'

expectations.text: Flags missing workspaceId scope on getById
expectations.evidence: No finding about getById/markPaid missing workspaceId scoping. The cross-tenant leak is not surfaced.

expectations.text: Flags zod imported in the domain contracts file
expectations.evidence: Does the opposite: Finding 6 suggests deriving types via z.infer<typeof InvoiceSchema>, i.e. keeping Zod in the contract. The domain zero-import rule is not raised.

expectations.text: Uses DevDigest layer vocabulary (Container, vendor/shared/contracts, toDomain, getContext, rule files)
expectations.evidence: Uses onion layers, DI, secrets rule, composition root, @devdigest/shared alias — strong generic vocabulary (no rule-file citations, no toDomain/getContext).
